keyPoints: An OS manages hardware resources (CPU, memory, storage, I/O) to provide a stable and efficient environment for applications.
OS structures (monolithic, microkernel, layered) define how the resource management components are organized and interact.
The primary goal of resource management is to allocate resources fairly, efficiently, and safely among competing processes.
commonMistakes: Confusing the OS's role in resource management with the resources themselves (e.g., the OS manages the CPU, it is not the CPU).
Assuming all OSs have the same internal structure (monolithic vs. microkernel).
Believing resource allocation is only about fairness and not also about security and preventing deadlock.
